content.method: Segregation index (dissimilarity/isolation indices) comparison before and after consolidation events across a sample of Hungarian districts, disaggregated by consolidation type, paired with enrollment-flow tracking to church-run versus state schools.
content.question: What is the net segregation effect of consolidating village schools into hub/urban schools—does it reduce isolated single-class segregation or increase it via non-Roma family flight to church or alternative schools—and does this vary by consolidation model (full closure, grade-split, networked hub)?
content.why_it_matters: T1, T3, T4, and T5 all carry unresolved segregation risk claims; T5 explicitly targets equity mitigation but cannot be evaluated without knowing the actual sorting effect, and T3's age-based split may relocate rather than resolve sorting.
